description: Thorium Reader is a free and open-source EPUB reading application designed with accessibility as a core feature. This is the desktop version supporting Windows, macOS, and Linux platforms.
evaluationMethodology: This report documents conformance evaluation of Thorium Reader Desktop version 3 against WCAG 2.2, UAAG 2.0, EPUB Accessibility 1.1, and DAISY User Requirements Standards. The evaluation methodology included: automated testing using accessibility scanning tools, manual accessibility review by accessibility professionals, user testing with assistive technologies (NVDA, JAWS on Windows; VoiceOver on macOS; Orca on Linux), functional testing with various EPUB publications, and keyboard navigation testing.
conformanceLevel: UAAG 2.0 Level AA, WCAG 2.2 Level AA, EPUB Accessibility 1.1
conformanceSummary: Thorium Reader Desktop provides comprehensive access to assistive technologies and numerous options to customize content rendering and interface behavior. The application achieves UAAG 2.0 Level AA and WCAG 2.2 Level AA conformance. In many scenarios, it meets Level AAA criteria. However, some limitations exist: interface zoom functionality can impact the reading experience, and a few specialized features remain untested.

strengths: Full keyboard navigation support with no keyboard traps
Excellent screen reader compatibility (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, Orca)
Comprehensive text configuration options (size, color, spacing, font)
Adjustable visual presentation and contrast settings
Full EPUB accessibility feature support (page lists, navigation documents, reading order)
limitations: WCAG 1.4.4 (Resize text): Viewport resizing without horizontal scrolling has limited implementation
Interface zoom without breaking reading experience requires refinement
Some specialized DAISY URS features not yet implemented (citation generation, tactile graphics support)

testingEnvironments: Testing with various EPUB publications from epubtest.org

changelog.description: Initial conformance report for Thorium Reader Desktop 3

productInfo.name: Thorium Reader Desktop
productInfo.description: Thorium Reader Desktop is a free, open-source EPUB reading application available for Windows, macOS, and Linux. The application is free, with no ads and no private data leaks. It is localized in a large set of languages.

conformanceOverview.conformanceLevel: Thorium Reader Desktop achieves UAAG 2.0 Level AA and WCAG 2.2 Level AA conformance. In many situations, it reaches AAA level criteria.
conformanceOverview.conformanceSummary: Thorium Reader Desktop provides full access to assistive technologies and numerous options to adjust content. In many situations, it reaches AAA level criterions. However, it has not yet been found how to provide interface zoom without breaking the reading experience.

testingMethodology.methodology: The evaluation included the following testing approaches:
testingApproaches: Assistive technology testing (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, Orca)
EPUB accessible reading features tested through epubtest.org

testedScenarios.scenario: Thorium Reader 3 with NVDA on Windows

uaag.title: Render Alternative Content
uaag.notes:
Abbr are rendered in a tooltip when they get focus.
  • Users can access `alt` text through TTS. However, that support can be enhanced by providing the `alt` text in the image viewing modal. See Issue #3139: Allow users to display alt texts to extend support for UAAG Guideline 1.1: Alternative content accessibility.
  • The video player allows to activate subtitles if they are available. Embed players are the responsibility of the ebook content creator.

uaag.title: Indicate Unrendered Alternative Content
uaag.notes:
• Abbr are indicated by a dotted underline.
  • alt text are not indicated. See Issue 3139: Allow users to display alt texts.
  • The video player indic ates the presence of subtitles if they are available. Embed players are the responsibility of the ebook content creator.

uaag.title: Replace Non-Text Content
uaag.notes: Caption Clean view is available for TTS reading. It should be extended to visual reading (see Issue #3141 Extend Caption / Clean View to visual reading (UAAG1.1.3 Replace Non-Text Content))

uaag.title: Facilitate Clear Display of Alternative Content for Time-based Media
uaag.notes: The video player indicates the presence of subtitles if they are available. Embed players are the responsibility of the ebook content creator.

uaag.title: Use Configurable Text for Time-based Media Captions
Allow Resize and Reposition of Time-based Media Alternatives
Support User Stylesheet or User Style Modification Mechanism
Move Viewport to Selection and Focus
Allow Zoom
uaag.notes: Additional Info: Reflowable contents can be adjusted in size and fixed layout contents can be zoomed. However the text from the interface cannot be resized. This is tracked in Issue Make the interface scalable

uaag.title: Allow Top-Level Viewport Open on Request
Source View
uaag.notes: Source view is available only with development versions, without LCP protection support.

uaag.title: Allow Direct Navigation to Enabled Elements
Allow Direct Activation of Enabled Elements
Present Direct Commands from Rendered Content
Present Direct Commands in User Interface
Alert on Wrap or No Match
Provide Structural Navigation by Heading and within Tables
Allow Multiple Sets of Preference Settings
Allow Preference Changes from outside the User Interface
Customize Display of Controls for User Interface Commands, Functions, and Add-ons
Adjustable Playback Rate for Prerecorded Content
Navigation of Time-Based Media by Time
Navigation of Time-Based Media by Semantics
Settings Changes can be Reversed or Confirmed
Implement Accessibility Features of Web Content Technology Specifications
Implement Accessibility Features of the Platform
Allow Content Elements to be Rendered in Alternative Viewers
Enable Reporting of User Agent Accessibility Faults

wcag.title: Non-text Content
wcag.notes: all visual information is also available as text
wcag.description: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose.

wcag.title: Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)
wcag.notes: Thorium Reader can restitue publications with pre-recorded audio or video.
wcag.description: Either an alternative for time-based media or an audio track is provided that presents equivalent information for prerecorded video-only content.

wcag.title: Captions (Prerecorded)
wcag.notes: Thorium Reader can restitue publications with pre-recorded captions.
wcag.description: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media, except when the media is a media alternative for text and is clearly labeled as such.

wcag.title: Captions (Live)
wcag.notes: Live captions may be available depending on the video-service provided by the ebook creator.
wcag.description: Captions are provided for all live audio content in synchronized media.

wcag.title: Audio Description (Prerecorded)
wcag.notes: Thorium Reader can restitue publications with pre-recorded Audio descriptions.
wcag.description: Audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.

wcag.title: Sign Language (Prerecorded)
wcag.notes: Thorium Reader can restitue publications with pre-recorded Sign language videos. An experimental feature allows the reading of authored-synchronisation between text and video.
wcag.description: Sign language interpretation is provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.

wcag.title: Extended Audio Description (Prerecorded)
wcag.notes: Thorium Reader can restitue publications with pre-recorded extended audio descriptions.
wcag.description: Where pauses in foreground audio are insufficient to allow audio descriptions to convey the sense of the video, extended audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.

wcag.title: Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
wcag.notes: Thorium Reader can restitue publications with pre-recorded media alternative.
wcag.description: An alternative for time-based media is provided for all prerecorded synchronized media and for all prerecorded video-only media.

wcag.title: Audio-only (Live)
wcag.notes: Thorium Reader can restitue publications with live-audio only, depending of the streaming application chosen and implemented by the ebook-creator.
wcag.description: An alternative for time-based media that presents equivalent information for live audio-only content is provided.

wcag.title: Info and Relationships
wcag.notes: Thorium Reader makes use of HTML markup with additional ARIA roles when necessary. It also fully restitues all XHTML and ARIA markup as authored in publications.
wcag.description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text.

wcag.title: Meaningful Sequence
wcag.description: When the sequence in which content is presented affects its meaning, a correct reading sequence can be programmatically determined.

wcag.title: Sensory Characteristics
wcag.description: Instructions provided for understanding and operating content do not rely solely on sensory characteristics of components such as shape, color, size, visual location, orientation, or sound.

wcag.title: Orientation
wcag.description: Content does not restrict its view and operation to a single display orientation, such as portrait or landscape, unless a specific display orientation is essential.

wcag.title: Identify Input Purpose
wcag.description: The purpose of each input field collecting information about the user can be programmatically determined when the input field serves a purpose identified in the Input Purposes for user interface components section.

wcag.title: Identify Purpose
wcag.description: In content implemented using markup languages, the purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined.

wcag.title: Use of Color
wcag.description: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information, indicating an action, prompting a response, or distinguishing a visual element.

wcag.title: Audio Control
wcag.description: If any audio on a web page plays automatically for more than 3 seconds, either a mechanism is available to pause or stop the audio, or a mechanism is available to control audio volume independently from the overall system volume level.

wcag.title: Contrast (Minimum)
wcag.description: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1, except for large text (3:1), incidental, or logotypes.

wcag.title: Resize text
wcag.notes: The text from the interface cannot be resized. This is tracked in Issue Make the interface scalable
wcag.description: Except for captions and images of text, text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent without loss of content or functionality.

wcag.title: Images of Text
wcag.description: If the technologies being used can achieve the visual presentation, text is used to convey information rather than images of text except for customizable or essential cases.

wcag.title: Contrast (Enhanced)
wcag.description: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 7:1, except for large text (4.5:1), incidental, or logotypes.

wcag.title: Low or No Background Audio
wcag.description: For prerecorded audio-only content that contains primarily speech in the foreground, is not an audio CAPTCHA or audio logo, and is not vocalization intended to be primarily musical expression such as singing or rapping, at least one of the following is true: no background, turn off, or 20 dB.

wcag.title: Visual Presentation
wcag.description: For the visual presentation of blocks of text, a mechanism is available to achieve the following: foreground and background colors can be selected, width is no more than 80 characters or glyphs, text is not justified, line spacing is at least space-and-a-half within paragraphs, paragraph spacing is at least 1.5 times larger than line spacing, and text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent.

wcag.title: Images of Text (No Exception)
wcag.description: Images of text are only used for pure decoration or where a particular presentation of text is essential to the information being conveyed.

wcag.title: Reflow
wcag.notes: The text from the interface cannot be resized. This is tracked in Issue Make the interface scalable
wcag.description: Content can be presented without loss of information or functionality, and without requiring scrolling in two dimensions for vertical scrolling content at 320 CSS pixels width or horizontal scrolling content at 256 CSS pixels height.

wcag.title: Non-text Contrast
wcag.description: The visual presentation of user interface components and graphical objects has a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent color(s).

wcag.title: Text Spacing
wcag.description: In content implemented using markup languages that support the following text style properties, no loss of content or functionality occurs by setting line height to at least 1.5 times the font size, spacing following paragraphs to at least 2 times the font size, letter spacing to at least 0.12 times the font size, and word spacing to at least 0.16 times the font size.

wcag.title: Content on Hover or Focus
wcag.description: Where receiving and then removing pointer hover or keyboard focus triggers additional content to become visible and then hidden, the following are true: dismissible, hoverable, persistent.

wcag.title: Keyboard
wcag.description: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes, except where the underlying function requires input that depends on the path of the user's movement and not just the endpoints.

wcag.title: No Keyboard Trap
wcag.description: If keyboard focus can be moved to a component of the page using a keyboard interface, then focus can be moved away from that component using only a keyboard interface, and, if it requires more than unmodified arrow or tab keys or other standard exit methods, the user is advised of the method for moving focus away.

wcag.title: Keyboard (No Exception)
wcag.description: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes.

wcag.title: Character Key Shortcuts
wcag.description: If a keyboard shortcut is implemented in content using only letter (including upper- and lower-case letters), punctuation, number, or symbol characters, then at least one of the following is true: turn off, remap, or active only on focus.

wcag.title: Timing Adjustable
wcag.description: For each time limit that is set by the content, at least one of the following is true: turn off, adjust, extend, real-time exception, essential exception, or 20 hour exception.

wcag.title: Pause, Stop, Hide
wcag.description: For moving, blinking, scrolling, or auto-updating information, a mechanism for the user to pause, stop, or hide it is available unless the movement is part of an activity where it is essential.

wcag.title: No Timing
wcag.description: Timing is not an essential part of the event or activity presented by the content, except for non-interactive synchronized media and real-time events.

wcag.title: Interruptions
wcag.description: Interruptions can be postponed or suppressed by the user, except interruptions involving an emergency.

wcag.title: Re-authenticating
wcag.description: When an authenticated session expires, the user can continue the activity without loss of data after re-authenticating.

wcag.title: Timeouts
wcag.description: Users are warned of the duration of any user inactivity that could cause data loss, unless the data is preserved for more than 20 hours when the user does not take any actions.

wcag.title: Three Flashes or Below Threshold
wcag.description: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period, or the flash is below the general flash and red flash thresholds.

wcag.title: Three Flashes
wcag.description: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period.

wcag.title: Animation from Interactions
wcag.description: Motion animation triggered by interaction can be disabled, unless the animation is essential to the functionality or information being conveyed.

wcag.title: Bypass Blocks
wcag.description: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple web pages.

wcag.title: Page Titled
wcag.description: Web pages have titles that describe topic or purpose.

wcag.title: Focus Order
wcag.description: If a web page can be navigated sequentially and the navigation sequences affect meaning or operation, focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability.

wcag.title: Link Purpose (In Context)
wcag.description: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone or from the link text together with its programmatically determined link context, except where the purpose of the link would be ambiguous to users in general.

wcag.title: Multiple Ways
wcag.description: More than one way is available to locate a web page within a set of web pages except where the web page is the result of, or a step in, a process.

wcag.title: Headings and Labels
wcag.description: Headings and labels describe topic or purpose.

wcag.title: Focus Visible
wcag.description: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.

wcag.title: Location
wcag.description: Information about the user's location within a set of web pages is available.

wcag.title: Link Purpose (Link Only)
wcag.description: A mechanism is available to allow the purpose of each link to be identified from link text alone, except where the purpose of the link would be ambiguous to users in general.

wcag.title: Section Headings
wcag.description: Section headings are used to organize the content.

wcag.title: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
wcag.description: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, the component is not entirely hidden behind author-created content.

wcag.title: Focus Not Obscured (Enhanced)
wcag.description: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, no part of the component is hidden behind author-created content.

wcag.title: Focus Appearance
wcag.description: When the keyboard focus indicator is visible, an area of the focus indicator meets the following: is at least as large as the area of a 2 CSS pixel thick perimeter of the unfocused component or sub-component, and has a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 between the same pixels in the focused and unfocused states.

wcag.title: Pointer Gestures
wcag.description: All functionality that uses multipoint or path-based gestures for operation can be operated with a single pointer without a path-based gesture, unless a multipoint or path-based gesture is essential.

wcag.title: Pointer Cancellation
wcag.description: For functionality that can be operated using a single pointer, at least one of the following is true: no down-event, abort or undo, up reversal, or essential.

wcag.title: Label in Name
wcag.description: For user interface components with labels that include text or images of text, the name contains the text that is presented visually.

wcag.title: Target Size (Enhanced)
wcag.description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels except when equivalent, inline, user agent control, or essential.

wcag.title: Concurrent Input Mechanisms
wcag.description: Web content does not restrict use of input modalities available on a platform except where the restriction is essential, required to ensure the security of the content, or required to respect user settings.

wcag.title: Dragging Movements
wcag.description: All functionality that uses a dragging movement for operation can be achieved by a single pointer without dragging, unless dragging is essential or the functionality is determined by the user agent and not modified by the author.

wcag.title: Target Size (Minimum)
wcag.description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels, except when spacing, equivalent, inline, user agent control, or essential.

wcag.title: Language of Page
wcag.description: The default human language of each web page can be programmatically determined.

wcag.title: Language of Parts
wcag.description: The human language of each passage or phrase in the content can be programmatically determined except for proper names, technical terms, words of indeterminate language, and words or phrases that have become part of the vernacular of the immediately surrounding text.

wcag.title: Unusual Words
wcag.notes: There is an ongoing discussion about Dictionary Integration in Thorium Reader
wcag.description: A mechanism is available for identifying specific definitions of words or phrases used in an unusual or restricted way, including idioms and jargon.

wcag.title: Abbreviations
wcag.notes: If the abbr tag is present in a puiblication, it will be rendered as a tooltip and made available to assistive technologies.
wcag.description: A mechanism for identifying the expanded form or meaning of abbreviations is available.

wcag.title: Reading Level
wcag.description: When text requires reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level after removal of proper names and titles, supplemental content, or a version that does not require reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level, is available.

wcag.title: Pronunciation
wcag.description: A mechanism is available for identifying specific pronunciation of words where meaning of the words, in context, is ambiguous without knowing the pronunciation.

wcag.title: On Focus
wcag.description: When any user interface component receives focus, it does not initiate a change of context.

wcag.title: On Input
wcag.description: Changing the setting of any user interface component does not automatically cause a change of context unless the user has been advised of the behavior before using the component.

wcag.title: Consistent Navigation
wcag.description: Navigational mechanisms that are repeated on multiple web pages within a set of web pages occur in the same relative order each time they are repeated, unless a change is initiated by the user.

wcag.title: Consistent Identification
wcag.description: Components that have the same functionality within a set of web pages are identified consistently.

wcag.title: Change on Request
wcag.description: Changes of context are initiated only by user request or a mechanism is available to turn off such changes.

wcag.title: Consistent Help
wcag.description: If a web page contains any help mechanisms, and those mechanisms are repeated on multiple web pages within a set of web pages, they occur in the same order relative to other page content, unless a change is initiated by the user.

wcag.title: Error Identification
wcag.description: If an input error is automatically detected, the item that is in error is identified and the error is described to the user in text.

wcag.title: Labels or Instructions
wcag.description: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input.

wcag.title: Error Suggestion
wcag.description: If an input error is automatically detected and suggestions for correction are known, then the suggestions are provided to the user, unless it would jeopardize the security or purpose of the content.

wcag.title: Error Prevention (Legal, Financial, Data)
wcag.description: For web pages that cause legal commitments or financial transactions for the user to occur, that modify or delete user-controllable data in data storage systems, or that submit user test responses, at least one of the following is true: reversible, checked, or confirmed.

wcag.title: Help
wcag.description: Context-sensitive help is available.

wcag.title: Error Prevention (All)
wcag.description: For web pages that require the user to submit information, at least one of the following is true: reversible, checked, or confirmed.

wcag.title: Redundant Entry
wcag.description: Information previously entered by or provided to the user that is required to be entered again in the same process is either auto-populated, or available for the user to select.

wcag.title: Accessible Authentication
wcag.description: A cognitive function test is not required for any step in an authentication process unless that step provides at least one of the following: alternative, mechanism, object recognition, or personal content.

wcag.title: Accessible Authentication (No Exception)
wcag.description: A cognitive function test is not required for any step in an authentication process unless that step provides at least one of the following: alternative or mechanism.

wcag.title: Name, Role, Value
wcag.description: For all user interface components, the name and role can be programmatically determined; states, properties, and values that can be set by the user can be programmatically set; and notification of changes to these items is available to user agents, including assistive technologies.

wcag.title: Status Messages
wcag.description: In content implemented using markup languages, status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties such that they can be presented to the user by assistive technologies without receiving focus.

epub.title: Discoverability: accessModeSufficient
epub.description: EPUB publications MUST include accessModeSufficient metadata indicating one or more access modes sufficient to consume content without significant loss of information.

epub.title: Discoverability: accessibilityFeature
epub.description: EPUB publications MUST include accessibilityFeature metadata listing features and adaptations that contribute to accessibility (e.g., alternative text, extended descriptions, captions).

epub.title: Discoverability: accessibilityHazard
epub.description: EPUB publications MUST include accessibilityHazard metadata identifying any potential hazards such as flashing, motion simulation, or sound.

epub.title: Discoverability: accessMode
epub.description: EPUB publications SHOULD include accessMode metadata specifying sensory perceptual systems required to process content (textual, visual, auditory, tactile).

epub.title: Discoverability: accessibilitySummary
epub.description: EPUB publications SHOULD include accessibilitySummary metadata providing human-readable summary of accessibility complementing other metadata.

epub.title: WCAG Conformance: Minimum Level
epub.description: EPUB publications MUST meet the requirements of WCAG 2.0 Level A at minimum, though WCAG 2.2 Level AA is strongly recommended.

epub.title: Page Navigation: Pagination Source
epub.description: When EPUB includes page navigation, the source of static page break locations must be identified with recognizable identifier such as ISBN or ISSN.

epub.title: Page Navigation: Page List
epub.description: EPUB publications with page navigation MUST include a page list providing navigation to static page break locations.

epub.title: Page Navigation: Page Breaks
epub.description: EPUB publications with page navigation SHOULD include page break markers identifying all pages reproduced from the source.

epub.title: Synchronized Text-Audio: Completeness
epub.description: EPUB with synchronized text-audio playback MUST include audio playback for all visible textual content and textual alternatives for visual media.

epub.title: Synchronized Text-Audio: Reading Order
epub.description: Synchronized text-audio playback instructions SHOULD reflect spine order of documents and element order within documents, or result in logical playback.

epub.title: Synchronized Text-Audio: Skippability
epub.description: Synchronized text-audio playback instructions SHOULD identify all skippable structures enabling users to skip secondary content.

epub.title: Synchronized Text-Audio: Escapability
epub.description: Synchronized text-audio playback instructions SHOULD identify all escapable structures enabling users to exit from structured content.

epub.title: Synchronized Text-Audio: Navigation Document
epub.description: EPUB with synchronized text-audio playback SHOULD include synchronized playback for the EPUB navigation document.

epub.title: Conformance Reporting: Publication Conformance
epub.description: EPUB publications MUST specify conformance using dcterms:conformsTo property in format: 'EPUB Accessibility 1.2 - WCAG [VERSION] Level [LEVEL]'.

epub.title: Conformance Reporting: Evaluator Name
epub.description: Package document metadata MUST include a11y:certifiedBy property specifying name of party that evaluated the EPUB publication.

epub.title: Conformance Reporting: Evaluation Date
epub.description: If evaluation date is known, include dcterms:date property conforming to ISO 8601 format associated with evaluator information.

epub.title: Conformance Reporting: Evaluator Credentials
epub.description: If evaluator has credentials, include a11y:certifierCredential properties associated with evaluator information.

epub.title: Conformance Reporting: Evaluator Report
epub.description: If evaluator provides public assessment report, provide link using a11y:certifierReport property associated with evaluator information.

epub.title: Accessibility Feedback
epub.description: EPUB publications MAY include accessibility contact email using a11y:contactEmail property for users to report issues.

daisyURS.title: Navigation: Forward and Backward
daisyURS.description: User must have straightforward way to navigate forward and backward through content (text or audio-based).

daisyURS.title: Navigation: Table of Contents
daisyURS.description: User must be able to navigate publication using Table of Contents with focus matching current reading position, supporting hierarchical/nested structures.

daisyURS.title: Navigation: By Pages
daisyURS.description: User must have way to navigate content by page numbers; may include pseudo-pages for publications without page markup.

daisyURS.title: Navigation: Audio Location
daisyURS.description: User must have way to go to specific location in audio-based content via time, percentage, or other approach.

daisyURS.title: Navigation: Return to Last Location
daisyURS.description: User must be able to return to last reading location when reopening publication; automatic saving required.

daisyURS.title: Navigation: Determine Current Location
daisyURS.description: User must be able to determine current position in content; minimum information is percentage progress.

daisyURS.title: Navigation: Return to Previous Location
daisyURS.description: User must be able to go back to previous location in content.

daisyURS.title: Screen Reader: User Interface
daisyURS.description: User must be able to use screen reader in user interface; all components must provide semantic labels and roles per WCAG 2.1 and ARIA.

daisyURS.title: Screen Reader: Navigation
daisyURS.description: Screen reader users must be able to navigate through content by headings, block items, lines, words and characters.

daisyURS.title: Screen Reader: Continuous Reading
daisyURS.description: Screen reader users must be able to read continuously from current position, pause, and resume from paused location.

daisyURS.title: Screen Reader: Semantic Markup
daisyURS.description: User must be able to leverage screen reader's ability to interpret semantic markup; headings, lists, tables, images must be exposed.

daisyURS.title: Screen Reader: Scrolling Mode
daisyURS.description: User must be able to use screen reader in scrolling mode.

daisyURS.title: Screen Reader: Internal Hyperlinks
daisyURS.description: Screen reader users must be able to navigate confidently between internal hyperlinks and read from new navigation position.

daisyURS.title: Screen Reader: Actionable Content
daisyURS.description: Screen reader users must be able to activate actionable content such as links, buttons, expandable elements.

daisyURS.title: Screen Reader: Footnotes/Endnotes
daisyURS.description: Screen reader users must be able to detect footnote references, reach footnote content, read it, and return to original position.

daisyURS.title: Screen Reader: Additional Navigation
daisyURS.description: User could use additional navigation features provided by reading app such as navigation by heading, landmarks, tables, figures, math.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Text-to-Speech
daisyURS.description: User must be able to listen to text-based content using text-to-speech; seamless transition between embedded audio, TTS, and screen reader.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Starting Position
daisyURS.description: User must control starting position of read aloud; starting from screen reader position, indicated position, or reading position.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Selected Content
daisyURS.description: User must be able to select text (word, phrase, sentence, paragraph) and have it read aloud.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Continuous Reading
daisyURS.description: User must listen to read aloud without manually advancing; continuous until end or user interruption.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Logical Reading Order
daisyURS.description: Read aloud must follow logical reading order of content rather than visual layout.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Appropriate Pauses
daisyURS.description: Read aloud must use appropriate pauses after headings, paragraphs, list items rather than continuous reading.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: View Text
daisyURS.description: User must see text being read aloud with automatic display updates; text highlighted may move to different part while read aloud continues.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Visual Emphasis
daisyURS.description: User must be able to visually emphasize text being read aloud and turn feature off.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Emphasis Customization
daisyURS.description: User must be able to change color or style of visual emphasis; could adjust number of words highlighted.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Language
daisyURS.description: User must hear read aloud in correct language when reading content with language tags; could override language switching.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Playback Speed
daisyURS.description: User must control playback speed of read aloud; speed adjustments maintain natural pitch and pronunciation.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Voice Control
daisyURS.description: User must be able to control voice of read aloud.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Image Alt Text
daisyURS.description: User must be able to listen to image alt text included in read aloud.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Math Content
daisyURS.description: User must be able to listen to math content when using read aloud.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Locked Device
daisyURS.description: User must be able to lock mobile device and use read aloud.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Media Controls
daisyURS.description: User must control read-aloud playback using device media controls (play, pause, skip, stop).

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Tables
daisyURS.description: User must use read aloud with tables; must read left to right, top to bottom.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Expandable Content
daisyURS.description: User must use read aloud with expandable/collapsible content; expanded content read, collapsed not read.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Escape Structures
daisyURS.description: User must escape from certain structures when using read aloud and continue from following item.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Skip Content
daisyURS.description: User must configure app to not announce skippable elements.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Reading Modes
daisyURS.description: User could choose from range of read aloud modes such as word, sentence, paragraph, page, chapter.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Pause Length
daisyURS.description: User could adjust pause length between content blocks during read aloud.

daisyURS.title: Read Aloud: Semantic Expressiveness
daisyURS.description: User could choose to have semantic expressiveness for read aloud.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Playback
daisyURS.description: User must access and play pre-recorded audio; seamless transition between embedded audio, TTS read aloud, and screen reader.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Starting Position
daisyURS.description: User must control starting position of embedded audio via time, percentage, or other approach.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Continuous Playback
daisyURS.description: User must listen to embedded audio without manually advancing; continuous playback with optional sleep timer.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Logical Reading Order
daisyURS.description: User must listen to embedded audio in correct logical reading order matching publication structure.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Synchronized Text
daisyURS.description: User must view corresponding text while listening to embedded audio if synchronized text available.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Visual Emphasis Toggle
daisyURS.description: User must enable or disable visual emphasis of synchronized text during embedded audio playback.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Emphasis Customization
daisyURS.description: User must customize color and style of visual emphasis; could adjust number of words highlighted.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Playback Speed
daisyURS.description: User must adjust playback speed without distortion; speed adjustments maintain natural pitch and pronunciation.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Alt Text
daisyURS.description: User must listen to alt texts included in embedded audio and skip if supported.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Math Content
daisyURS.description: User must listen to math content included in embedded audio if present.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Locked Device
daisyURS.description: User must lock mobile device and continue listening to embedded audio.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Device Controls
daisyURS.description: User must control embedded audio playback using device-level media controls.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Table Structure
daisyURS.description: User must listen to embedded audio that conveys structure and content of tables.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Expandable Content
daisyURS.description: User must listen to embedded audio reflecting current state of expandable content; only expanded content plays.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Escape Structures
daisyURS.description: User can escape from defined structures during embedded audio playback and resume from following item.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Skip Content
daisyURS.description: User must configure embedded audio playback to skip content marked as skippable.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Selected Portion
daisyURS.description: User could play embedded audio for selected portion of text if content is synchronized.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Playback Modes
daisyURS.description: User could choose different playback modes for embedded audio including non-continuous playback.

daisyURS.title: Embedded Audio: Pause Length
daisyURS.description: User could adjust pause length between content blocks during embedded audio playback.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Typeface
daisyURS.description: User must change typeface of all text, choosing from sans serif and serif fonts.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Line Spacing
daisyURS.description: User must be able to control line spacing of text.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Letter Spacing
daisyURS.description: User must be able to control letter spacing.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Text Justification
daisyURS.description: User must turn off justification and center alignment of blocks of text.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Font Size
daisyURS.description: User must be able to change font size.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Margins and Line Length
daisyURS.description: User must change margins and adjust line length for blocks of text.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Scrolling View
daisyURS.description: User must view reflowable content in scrolling view.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Platform Features
daisyURS.description: User must use app with high contrast and magnification features of operating system platform.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Math Expressions
daisyURS.description: User must adjust size and color of math expressions by adjusting text's font size and color.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Background and Foreground Colors
daisyURS.description: User must personalize background and foreground colors.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Brightness
daisyURS.description: User must change display brightness.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Hide Content
daisyURS.description: User must visually hide certain content such as page breaks and footnotes.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Text Styling
daisyURS.description: User should remove visual text styling (underline, italic, bold).

daisyURS.title: Visual: Reading Emphasis
daisyURS.description: User should visually emphasize text being read using highlight, ruler lines, or other means.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Math Enlargement
daisyURS.description: User should enlarge math expressions for closer inspection.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Capitalization
daisyURS.description: User could change capitalization of text to sentence style.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Pagination
daisyURS.description: User could view content in paginated view.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Math Color
daisyURS.description: User could display mathematical expressions without color formatting using consistent text color.

daisyURS.title: Visual: Additional Features
daisyURS.description: Additional visual adjustment features provided by reading app.

daisyURS.title: Bookmarking: Create
daisyURS.description: User must create bookmarks at arbitrary positions within content.

daisyURS.title: Bookmarking: Delete
daisyURS.description: User must delete bookmarks individually; could delete all bookmarks at once.

daisyURS.title: Bookmarking: Overview
daisyURS.description: User must view overview of all bookmarks sorted by appearance order and navigate from there.

daisyURS.title: Bookmarking: Identify
daisyURS.description: User must identify each bookmark via meaningful system-generated title; should be editable or taggable.

daisyURS.title: Bookmarking: Auto-Save
daisyURS.description: User bookmarks must be saved automatically including during offline use.

daisyURS.title: Highlighting: Create
daisyURS.description: User must create, review, edit and delete highlights.

daisyURS.title: Highlighting: Distinguish
daisyURS.description: User must distinguish highlights.

daisyURS.title: Highlighting: Hide
daisyURS.description: User should hide highlights.

daisyURS.title: Highlighting: Change
daisyURS.description: User must change color or category of highlights.

daisyURS.title: Highlighting: Navigate
daisyURS.description: User should navigate from highlight to highlight.

daisyURS.title: Highlighting: Auto-Save
daisyURS.description: User highlights must be saved automatically including during offline use.

daisyURS.title: Highlighting: Export
daisyURS.description: User should export highlighted sections.

daisyURS.title: Notes: Create
daisyURS.description: User must create, review, edit and delete notes during reading.

daisyURS.title: Notes: View in Context
daisyURS.description: User must view notes in context such as margin or overlay maintaining reading position.

daisyURS.title: Notes: Auto-Save
daisyURS.description: User notes must be saved automatically including during offline use.

daisyURS.title: Notes: Navigate
daisyURS.description: Screen reader users should navigate from one note to another.

daisyURS.title: Notes: Formatting
daisyURS.description: User should apply basic formatting to note content (bold, italic, underline).

daisyURS.title: Notes: Visibility
daisyURS.description: User should hide or show notes.

daisyURS.title: Notes: Read Aloud
daisyURS.description: User should have notes read aloud individually or in sequence.

daisyURS.title: Notes: Export
daisyURS.description: User should export notes in structured format.

daisyURS.title: Notes: Format Variety
daisyURS.description: User could create notes in formats beyond text such as handwriting, math notation, or video.

daisyURS.title: Answers: Input
daisyURS.description: User must enter, edit and review answers into input fields embedded within publication.

daisyURS.title: Answers: Save
daisyURS.description: User must save answers automatically; ideally online with offline support and syncing.

daisyURS.title: Answers: View Previous
daisyURS.description: User must see previously saved answers when reopening book.

daisyURS.title: Answers: Navigate Fields
daisyURS.description: Screen reader users must navigate between input fields.

daisyURS.title: Answers: Text Formatting
daisyURS.description: User should format answers in text fields using basic rich text editor.

daisyURS.title: Answers: Share
daisyURS.description: User should share answers with others by export or within system.

daisyURS.title: Answers: Custom Fields
daisyURS.description: User could add custom input fields where none exist.

daisyURS.title: Answers: Synchronize
daisyURS.description: User could synchronize answers across multiple devices.

daisyURS.title: Answers: Auto-Check
daisyURS.description: User could have answers automatically checked.

daisyURS.title: Library: Login
daisyURS.description: User must have simple accessible way to log in to reading system.
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Library: Search and Browse
daisyURS.description: User must search, browse and acquire titles in service provider's collection.
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Library: Add Titles
daisyURS.description: User must add titles from diverse sources (sideload content).
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Library: Serial Subscriptions
daisyURS.description: User must subscribe to and unsubscribe from serial publications.
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Library: Remove Titles
daisyURS.description: User must be able to remove titles from bookshelf.
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Library: Download
daisyURS.description: User must be able to download titles and read offline.
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Library: Manage Sources
daisyURS.description: User must be able manage titles from multiple sources.
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Library: Manage Collection
daisyURS.description: User must manage diverse collection with search, sort, filter, categorization.
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Library: Synchronized Bookshelf
daisyURS.description: Bookshelf must automatically reflect changes made in synchronized services, syncing across devices.
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Library: Publication Preview
daisyURS.description: User should be able to preview publication when available.
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Library: Open EPUB from File Manager
daisyURS.description: User should be able to open EPUB files directly from file manager.
daisyURS.notes: this remains dependant of the feed provider

daisyURS.title: Misc: Documentation
daisyURS.description: User must have user-friendly documentation for reading system.

daisyURS.title: Misc: Error Messages
daisyURS.description: User must receive clear and user-friendly error messages.

daisyURS.title: Misc: Protected Content
daisyURS.description: User must access and read protected content using assistive technologies.

daisyURS.title: Misc: Citations
daisyURS.description: User should get citations or references in accepted formats.

daisyURS.title: Less distraction mode / Zen mode / Simplified interface
daisyURS.description: A simplified or 'Zen' mode should be available, hiding most interface controls and presenting only essential reading functions. This reduces cognitive load and visual distractions, benefiting users with attention-related or cognitive disabilities.

daisyURS.title: Configurable reading preferences
daisyURS.description: The application shall allow users to configure reading-related features both globally (via application settings) and locally (per document). Global settings (e.g. font size, contrast, voice speed) ensure consistency, while some features (e.g. layout or reading mode) may need to be overridden per document.

daisyURS.title: Follow external links
daisyURS.description: Some digital publications include hyperlinks to external web resources. Users should be able to follow these links and open them in an external web browser outside the reading system, supporting access to supplementary materials.

daisyURS.title: Parallel text viewing and navigation for linked content
daisyURS.description: Users should be able to view multiple versions of a text side by side, such as an original and a translation, or a text with annotations, supporting comparative reading and study.

daisyURS.title: Display word counts in tables of contents
daisyURS.description: The app should display the word count for each article or chapter in the table of contents, helping users estimate the length and effort required for each section.

daisyURS.title: Disclose text style properties to assistive technologies
daisyURS.description: Text style properties such as font, line spacing, indentation, justification, and emphasis (bold, italic, underline) should be made available to assistive technologies, allowing screen readers and braille displays to convey visual structure and emphasis in non-visual ways.

daisyURS.title: Turn hyphenation on/off
daisyURS.description: Users should be able to enable or disable hyphenation. This customization supports a more accessible and comfortable reading experience, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision, by reducing visual clutter and improving text flow.
daisyURS.notes: Hyphenation is turned off when text is not justified

daisyURS.title: Reflow of text for fixed layout as well
daisyURS.description: Text in fixed-layout documents should be able to reflow, allowing for better readability on small screens or when using magnification.

daisyURS.title: Regional navigation features
daisyURS.description: The app could provide region-based navigation which zooms in and steps the reader through a defined reading order. The reader could navigate between regions at their own pace.

daisyURS.title: Support for LLM inference or other processing
daisyURS.description: The app should allow integration of AI-powered features, such as summarization, question answering, or contextual assistance based on the content of the publication.
daisyURS.notes: This would be subject to respect of copyrighted content by third party AI providers.

daisyURS.title: Print or export specific content
daisyURS.description: Users should be able to print or export selected parts of a publication, such as templates or activity pages. This supports hands-on learning and accessibility for users who benefit from physical or tactile formats.

daisyURS.title: Export content for alternative format production
daisyURS.description: The app must allow users to export content in a digital format suitable for creating alternative accessible formats, such as braille or large print.

daisyURS.title: Support for tactile graphics
daisyURS.description: The app should, where feasible, support the rendering of tactile graphics via compatible hardware such as refreshable Braille displays or pin-matrix devices, enabling users with visual impairments to access graphical content.

daisyURS.title: Dynamic content based on user input
daisyURS.description: The user should be able to enter personal or contextual information—such as the number of servings in a recipe or a name for personalized narration—which then dynamically updates the content. This makes digital publications more engaging and helps reduce cognitive load.

daisyURS.title: Book recommendations based on personal preferences
daisyURS.description: The app should provide personalized book recommendations based on the user's reading history, interests, or profile, making it easier to discover relevant content.

daisyURS.title: Book recommendation sharing
daisyURS.description: Users should be able to share book recommendations with others, for example via social media or within the reading platform, to encourage community and engagement.

daisyURS.title: Borrowing history indicator
daisyURS.description: The app should indicate which books a user has previously borrowed or read, helping them avoid duplicates and revisit favorites.

daisyURS.title: Digital clippings archive
daisyURS.description: The app should allow users to save and organize excerpts or quotes from digital publications, functioning as a digital scrapbook for easy reference and study.
